conditions.hearingFindings: Variable. May involve sensorineural, conductive, mixed, neural, unilateral, bilateral, fluctuating, or progressive findings depending on the condition.
conditions.vestibularFindings: Possible when the condition involves the vestibular end organs, eighth nerve, central pathways, migraine mechanisms, third-window physiology, or multisensory balance.

troubleshooting.complaint: App controls do not match devices
troubleshooting.patientChecks: Check battery or charger, clean microphone openings gently, change wax filter or dome if trained, confirm device is inserted correctly, and note where the problem occurs.
troubleshooting.clinicianChecks: Verify device function, otoscopy, coupling, feedback manager status, real-ear or simulated verification as available, data logging, and patient description before changing programming.
troubleshooting.programming: Use manufacturer-neutral steps: compare to prescriptive target, check MPO and loudness growth, adjust gain by input level, and confirm comfort with speech and calibrated loudness samples.
troubleshooting.acousticDevice: Inspect coupling, receiver, dome, earmold, venting, tube, wax filter, microphone ports, moisture, and physical fit.
troubleshooting.redFlags: Pain, drainage, sudden hearing change, dizziness, unilateral change, or device-related injury warrants medical or urgent clinic guidance.
troubleshooting.documentation: Patient reported: [complaint]. Device and ear-level checks completed. Programming and acoustic changes reviewed with verification or functional confirmation when available.

troubleshooting.patientChecks: Check battery or charger, clean microphone openings gently, change wax filter or dome if trained, confirm device is inserted correctly, and note where the problem occurs.
troubleshooting.clinicianChecks: Verify device function, otoscopy, coupling, feedback manager status, real-ear or simulated verification as available, data logging, and patient description before changing programming.
troubleshooting.programming: Check MPO first, then loud-input gain and compression. Consider reducing MPO or loud-input gain in the frequency region triggering discomfort, reducing transient noise features if available, and adding a comfort program while maintaining speech audibility.
troubleshooting.acousticDevice: Inspect coupling, receiver, dome, earmold, venting, tube, wax filter, microphone ports, moisture, and physical fit.
troubleshooting.redFlags: Pain, drainage, sudden hearing change, dizziness, unilateral change, or device-related injury warrants medical or urgent clinic guidance.
troubleshooting.documentation: Patient reported: [complaint]. Device and ear-level checks completed. Programming and acoustic changes reviewed with verification or functional confirmation when available.

troubleshooting.patientChecks: Check battery or charger, clean microphone openings gently, change wax filter or dome if trained, confirm device is inserted correctly, and note where the problem occurs.

troubleshooting.programming: Check HA function and verified audibility, CI map comfort, loudness balance using speech and environmental sounds, streaming balance, delay perception, and whether low-frequency acoustic cues complement the CI. Adjust HA gain/coupling and CI levels cautiously.
troubleshooting.acousticDevice: Inspect external equipment, coil retention, magnet comfort, microphone covers, accessories, aided detection, and implant-center safety pathway.
troubleshooting.redFlags: Facial twitching, dizziness, pain, swelling, skin breakdown, sudden loss of sound, or new neurologic symptoms requires prompt implant-center or medical guidance.
troubleshooting.documentation: Patient reported: [complaint]. External equipment, datalogging, impedances, map history, and functional access were reviewed. Counseling and referral pathway documented.

patterns.name: ASSR follow-up recommendation pattern
patterns.typicalFindings: Findings may be consistent with the pattern when history, audiologic results, and cross-check measures align. Audiologic findings alone are not diagnostic.
patterns.symptoms: Symptoms vary and may include hearing difficulty, tinnitus, dizziness, imbalance, sound sensitivity, device difficulty, or listening fatigue.
patterns.tympanometry: May be normal or abnormal depending on outer-ear and middle-ear involvement. Interpret with otoscopy and history.
patterns.reflexes: May be present, elevated, absent, decayed, or inconsistent depending on the pattern. Use cautiously with other measures.
patterns.oaes: May be present, absent, reduced, or inconsistent depending on cochlear outer hair cell status and middle-ear transmission.
patterns.speech: Speech testing may confirm functional listening difficulty, reveal disproportionate word recognition, or show speech-in-noise deficits not predicted by the audiogram.
patterns.vestibular: Relevant when dizziness, vertigo, imbalance, falls, sound-induced dizziness, pressure-induced dizziness, or hearing-balance patterns are reported.
patterns.nextBattery: Consider otoscopy, immittance, pure-tone thresholds, speech testing, speech-in-noise, OAEs, acoustic reflexes, ABR, vestibular testing, aided testing, or medical referral depending on the pattern.
patterns.referral: Consider ENT, otology, neurology, genetics, vestibular PT, CI center, pediatrician, or urgent care based on red flags and scope.
patterns.counseling: Use cautious language: findings may be consistent with this pattern, additional testing may be needed, and medical diagnosis requires the appropriate provider.
patterns.notConfirm: This pattern does not confirm a medical diagnosis by itself.

research.title: Adult cochlear implant candidacy referral
research.clinicalUse: Zeitler et al. summarize evidence for referral of adults with bilateral hearing loss for formal CI evaluation.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Cochlear implantation for single-sided deafness
research.clinicalUse: ACI guidance reviews evidence relevant to adult CI assessment and management for single-sided deafness.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: AzBio sentence validation
research.clinicalUse: Spahr and colleagues validated sentence lists used in aided speech perception assessment.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Sudden sensorineural hearing loss guideline
research.clinicalUse: The AAO-HNSF guideline emphasizes prompt recognition and management of sudden hearing loss.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: BPPV clinical practice guideline
research.clinicalUse: The AAO-HNSF guideline supports accurate BPPV diagnosis and appropriate repositioning maneuvers.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Pediatric amplification guideline
research.clinicalUse: AAA guidance addresses selection, fitting, verification, and outcome assessment for pediatric amplification.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Early Hearing Detection and Intervention
research.clinicalUse: JCIH guidance supports universal access to screening, evaluation, and intervention systems.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Hearing aids for adults
research.clinicalUse: ASHA summarizes hearing aid fitting, verification, counseling, and outcome processes within aural rehabilitation.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Tinnitus education
research.authorsYear: National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders (2023)
research.clinicalUse: NIDCD defines tinnitus and summarizes causes and treatment directions for patients.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Meniere's disease education
research.authorsYear: National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders (2024)
research.clinicalUse: NIDCD describes Meniere's disease as an inner-ear disorder associated with vertigo, tinnitus, hearing loss, and fullness.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Otosclerosis education
research.authorsYear: National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders (2022)
research.clinicalUse: NIDCD describes otosclerosis as abnormal bone remodeling that can disrupt sound transmission.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Auditory neuropathy education
research.authorsYear: National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders (2018)
research.clinicalUse: NIDCD describes auditory neuropathy as a disorder in which the inner ear detects sound but neural transmission to the brain is impaired.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Congenital CMV and hearing loss
research.authorsYear: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (2024)
research.clinicalUse: CDC notes that congenital CMV hearing loss may be present at birth or develop later and may progress.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.

research.title: Genetic hearing loss overview
research.clinicalUse: GeneReviews summarizes genetic hearing loss and differentiates syndromic and nonsyndromic presentations.
research.limitations: Use in context of patient-specific findings and scope of practice.
